an1.575:0.2: 30. The Chapter on *Sati* Applied to the Body
an1.575:1.1: "Whoever, *bhikkhus*, has encompassed the great ocean with their mind [*citta*] — within that are contained all the streams that flow to the sea;
an1.575:1.2: just so, *bhikkhus*, whoever has developed and cultivated *sati* applied to the body — within that are contained all the wholesome *dhammas* belonging to true knowledge."
an1.576-582:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to great urgency … leads to great benefit … leads to great security from bondage … leads to *sati* and clear comprehension [*satisampajaññāya*] … leads to the attainment of knowledge and vision … leads to a pleasant abiding here and now … leads to the realization of the fruit of true knowledge and liberation.
an1.576-582:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to great urgency … leads to great benefit … leads to great security from bondage … leads to *sati* and clear comprehension [*satisampajaññāya*] … leads to the attainment of knowledge and vision … leads to a pleasant abiding here and now … leads to the realization of the fruit of true knowledge and liberation."
an1.583:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, the body is calmed, the mind [*citta*] is calmed, thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*] are stilled, and all *dhammas* belonging to true knowledge reach fulfillment in development [*bhāvanā*].
an1.583:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, the body is calmed, the mind [*citta*] is calmed, thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*] are stilled, and all *dhammas* belonging to true knowledge reach fulfillment in development [*bhāvanā*]."
an1.584:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, unwholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do not arise, and unwholesome *dhammas* that have arisen are abandoned.
an1.584:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, unwholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do not arise, and unwholesome *dhammas* that have arisen are abandoned."
an1.585:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, wholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do arise, and wholesome *dhammas* that have arisen lead to greater increase and expansion.
an1.585:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, wholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do arise, and wholesome *dhammas* that have arisen lead to greater increase and expansion."
an1.586-590:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, blindness [*avijjā*] is abandoned … true knowledge arises … the conceit 'I am' is abandoned … underlying tendencies are uprooted … fetters are abandoned.
an1.586-590:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, blindness [*avijjā*] is abandoned … true knowledge arises … the conceit 'I am' is abandoned … underlying tendencies are uprooted … fetters are abandoned."
an1.591-592:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the breakthrough of *paññā* … leads to final *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānāya*].
an1.591-592:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the breakthrough of *paññā* … leads to final *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānāya*]."
an1.593-595:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, there is penetration into many elements … penetration into diverse elements … analytical knowledge of many elements.
an1.593-595:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, there is penetration into many elements … penetration into diverse elements … analytical knowledge of many elements."
an1.596-599:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry … leads to the realization of the fruit of once-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of non-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of *arahantship*.
an1.596-599:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry … leads to the realization of the fruit of once-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of non-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of *arahantship*."
an1.600-615:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the attainment of *paññā* … leads to the growth of *paññā* … leads to the expansion of *paññā* … leads to great *paññā* … leads to broad *paññā* … leads to vast *paññā* … leads to deep *paññā* … leads to unrivalled *paññā* … leads to abundant *paññā* … leads to profuse *paññā* … leads to swift *paññā* … leads to quick *paññā* … leads to ready *paññā* … leads to rapid *paññā* … leads to keen *paññā* … leads to penetrating *paññā*.
an1.600-615:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the attainment of *paññā* … leads to the growth of *paññā* … leads to the expansion of *paññā* … leads to great *paññā* … leads to broad *paññā* … leads to vast *paññā* … leads to deep *paññā* … leads to unrivalled *paññā* … leads to abundant *paññā* … leads to profuse *paññā* … leads to swift *paññā* … leads to quick *paññā* … leads to ready *paññā* … leads to rapid *paññā* … leads to keen *paññā* … leads to penetrating *paññā*."
an1.600-615:1.5: (The Chapter on *Sati* Applied to the Body.)
